(November 13, 1792 – July 20, 1873) was an American businessman and politician from [New London, New Hampshire](/wiki/New_London,_New_Hampshire "New London, New Hampshire"). He owned and operated a grist mill and a stage line, and served as the 20th [Governor of New Hampshire](/wiki/Governor_of_New_Hampshire "Governor of New Hampshire") from 1846 to 1847.

**Anthony Colby** (November 13, 1792 – July 20, 1873) was an American businessman and politician from [New London, New Hampshire](/wiki/New_London,_New_Hampshire "New London, New Hampshire"). He owned and operated a grist mill and a stage line, and served as the 20th [Governor of New Hampshire](/wiki/Governor_of_New_Hampshire "Governor of New Hampshire") from 1846 to 1847.

## Biography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Anthony_Colby&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Biography")]

Colby was born in [New London, New Hampshire](/wiki/New_London,_New_Hampshire "New London, New Hampshire"), on November 13, 1792. He was educated locally and became a successful business owner and operator, with his ventures including a stagecoach line, gristmill, and factory for producing scythes.

He was also active in the militia, serving as an ensign during the [War of 1812](/wiki/War_of_1812 "War of 1812"), and attaining the rank of major general in 1837.

Colby entered politics as a member of the [New Hampshire House of Representatives](/wiki/New_Hampshire_House_of_Representatives "New Hampshire House of Representatives"), serving from 1828 to 1832 and 1837 to 1839. He was an unsuccessful candidate for Congress in 1833 and 1835, and ran unsuccessfully for governor in 1843, 1844 and 1845.

In 1846, Colby ran for governor as the [Whig](/wiki/Whig_Party_(United_States) "Whig Party (United States)") nominee. He originally placed second to Democrat [Jared W. Williams](/wiki/Jared_W._Williams "Jared W. Williams"), receiving 32% of the vote to Williams's 48%. However, the [state constitution](/wiki/Constitution_of_New_Hampshire "Constitution of New Hampshire") imposed a majority-vote requirement in gubernatorial elections, so the failure of any candidate in the race to win a majority threw the election to the [General Court](/wiki/New_Hampshire_General_Court "New Hampshire General Court"). A Whig–[Liberty Party](/wiki/Liberty_Party_(United_States,_1840) "Liberty Party (United States, 1840)")–Independent Democrat coalition, led by Independent Democrat [John P. Hale](/wiki/John_P._Hale "John P. Hale"), won control of the state government and elected Colby as Governor over Williams.[[1]](#cite_note-1) Colby served from June 4, 1846, to June 3, 1847. He ran unsuccessfully for re-election in 1847, in which he lost to Williams.

After leaving the governorship, Colby remained active in business, the military and politics. He served again in the New Hampshire House from 1860 to 1861, and during the [American Civil War](/wiki/American_Civil_War "American Civil War") served as [Adjutant General](/wiki/Adjutant_General_of_New_Hampshire "Adjutant General of New Hampshire") of the [New Hampshire Militia](/wiki/New_Hampshire_Militia "New Hampshire Militia") from 1861 to 1863. He then became provost marshal of the militia, with his son Daniel succeeding him as adjutant general.

Colby was interested in [higher education](/wiki/Higher_education "Higher education"). He was a trustee of [Dartmouth College](/wiki/Dartmouth_College "Dartmouth College") from 1850 to 1870, and received an [honorary](/wiki/Honorary_degree "Honorary degree") [Master of Arts](/wiki/Master_of_Arts "Master of Arts") from Dartmouth in 1850. He was also the founder of Colby Academy, which through expansions and mergers is now known as [Colby-Sawyer College](/wiki/Colby-Sawyer_College "Colby-Sawyer College").

Colby died in [New London](/wiki/New_London,_New_Hampshire "New London, New Hampshire") on July 20, 1873, and was buried in New London's Old Main Street Cemetery.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

Colby's papers are held at [Colby-Sawyer College](/wiki/Colby-Sawyer_College "Colby-Sawyer College").[[3]](#cite_note-3)
